greater-glasgow-life-sciences.alt: Diagnostic flow for Greater Glasgow Life Sciences: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, four subclusters, and outcome distribution.
greater-glasgow-life-sciences.caption: Anchored by the University of Glasgow (31%) and Strathclyde (20%), and reinforced by Glasgow Caledonian University, NHS Greater Glasgow & Clyde and the IBioIC industrial-biotech innovation centre, the cluster attracts £1.07bn of UKRI funding across 4,329 grants.

greater-glasgow-space.alt: Diagnostic flow for Greater Glasgow Space: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, subclusters, and outcome distribution.
greater-glasgow-space.caption: Largely a University of Strathclyde story — 86% of the region's £229m in UKRI space funding — supported by Glasgow-built satellite firms including Clyde Space, Craft Prospect and Wideblue, within one of Europe's leading small-satellite manufacturing hubs.

greater-glasgow-net-zero.alt: Diagnostic flow for Greater Glasgow Net Zero: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, subclusters, and outcome distribution.
greater-glasgow-net-zero.caption: Glasgow and Strathclyde lead the academic base of the cluster (29% and 20% respectively), with contributions from Nitech Solutions, ScottishPower and Loganair supporting £1.07bn of UKRI-funded work in decarbonisation, power systems and clean aviation.

greater-glasgow-digital-creative.alt: Diagnostic flow for Greater Glasgow Digital Creative: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, subclusters, and outcome distribution.
greater-glasgow-digital-creative.caption: The region's largest UKRI research cluster — £1.12bn across 4,344 grants — combines University of Glasgow (37%) and Strathclyde (27%) strengths in computing and AI with photonics and compound-semiconductor specialists including M Squared Lasers and CST Global.

greater-glasgow-advanced-manufacturing.alt: Diagnostic flow for Greater Glasgow Advanced Manufacturing: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, subclusters, and outcome distribution.
greater-glasgow-advanced-manufacturing.caption: Anchored by Strathclyde (38%) — with NMIS and AFRC underpinning much of that activity — the cluster connects Kelvin Nanotechnology, Weir Group, Nitech and Spirit AeroSystems into the Clyde's advanced manufacturing and aerostructures supply chain, representing £459m in UKRI funding.

greater-glasgow-fintech.alt: Diagnostic flow for Greater Glasgow Fintech: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, subclusters, and outcome distribution.
greater-glasgow-fintech.caption: While branded as fintech, the cluster is primarily research-led: the Universities of Glasgow (48%) and Strathclyde (36%) account for most UKRI funding, centred on data science, financial modelling, cyber security and risk analytics.

greater-glasgow.alt: Diagnostic flow for the Greater Glasgow innovation ecosystem: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, six clusters, and outcome distribution.
greater-glasgow.caption: Two anchor universities — Glasgow and Strathclyde — six interlocking research clusters, and a dense Clyde-corridor network of NHS hospitals, innovation centres and advanced supply-chain firms together account for £4.56bn of UKRI funding across 5,718 grants.

cta.framing: Same data examined through five diagnostic lenses — Pipeline, Leverage, Triple Helix, Throughput, Collaboration. The interactive diagnostic is currently in private preview.

source_line: Sources: UKRI Gateway to Research (grants, outcomes); OpenAlex (publications); Companies House (spin-out lifecycle); Crunchbase (disclosed capital). Snapshot May 2026.
